title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S19
body:
**Scene 19: 24:00–25:30 · The Visitor Explains**

HERO FRAME: Interior of the parlor, but warmer now · the brass lamp seems to glow more golden · two figures (Walter and Visitor, both seen from behind or in silhouette) sitting across the small table · the bottle and glass have been quietly moved aside · two teacups have appeared in their place

MOTION: kling — steam rises from the two teacups. The lamplight warms. The photograph of Walter's mother on the dresser seems to catch a soft glow. Walter's posture has loosened. The Visitor's hand (only hand visible) gestures gently toward the teacup.

MOOD: Hospitality across the impossible.

AUDIO: Visitor: *"You are not the last, Walter. There are many of us. We have been searching for you. We were quiet because we did not know if you were ready."*

Walter: *"Ready for what?"*

Visitor: *"For company. For home. For supper."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
